pattern: Only the ancestor chain of the single node holding genetic value 1 can ever have a missing value greater than 1, since every other subtree lacks 1 outright; walking that chain upward while accumulating each subtree's values into a running set lets the mex be tracked incrementally instead of recomputed per node.
hints: Every subtree that does not contain the node whose value is 1 automatically has 1 as its answer -- so first find that one node.
Only the ancestors of that node can possibly have an answer greater than 1; walk upward from it toward the root, one parent at a time.

At each step upward, add the newly-entered subtree's values (skipping the child subtree already accounted for) into a running set, then advance a smallest-missing-value counter forward through that set -- it only ever increases, so it never needs to restart from 1.

edge_inputs.description: value 1 sits at the root, so every node's answer depends on the full tree
value 1 is a leaf far from the root
a long path with value 1 at the deepest leaf
a star-shaped tree (root with many direct children), value 1 on a leaf, so only the root and that leaf are ancestors
value 1 node has its own children, which must be counted in its own subtree but must not be double-counted when moving to the parent
genetic values at the maximum bound, clustered near it
a skewed chain where the node with value 1 is the very last leaf, forcing the full-length ancestor walk

stress.description: a random tree built by attaching each node to a uniformly random earlier node, with a random distinct permutation of genetic values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    parents = [-1] + [rng.randint(0, i - 1) for i in range(1, n)]
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [parents, nums]

stress.description: a long random chain (path graph), stressing the ancestor-walk length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    parents = [-1] + list(range(0, n - 1))
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [parents, nums]
